source.title: How To Attack Freezer and Laundry on Oregon (Basement) - Rainbow Six Siege
source.notes: This video is a single dedicated strategy, not a full site-by-site walkthrough: it covers ONE alternate execute onto the Basement site (Ebox/Laundry/Freezer) via Freezer/Laundry rather than the standard Blue-door push. Only this one site/plan is covered; Top Floor and Meeting/Kitchen attacks are not discussed. Room/callout names are the creator's informal ones. Auto-generated captions badly mangled two operator names throughout ('ab Banner'/'a Banner'/'Havana'/'abana'/'haban' and 'Capal'/'Capow'/'Capao') — resolved with high confidence from gadget context (see notes below). One additional operator name ('doabe') could not be confidently resolved and is flagged as unconfirmed.

gadgetPlacements.location: Small Tower drone hole / tray in the corner of the kitchen
gadgetPlacements.note: Pre-place a drone here (or behind the tin/bucket near the drone hole as an alternative spot) at the start of the round. Watch the double door and the corridor — if nobody crosses either, nobody can be on that side of the map at all. Leave it here through the whole setup phase.
Jump the drone up onto the desk, then the gray cabinet, then the wooden cabinet, and leave it up there. Reveals anyone flanking down Main stairs and also sees the Meeting double door.
Drone placement that watches the Freezer flank, Top White, Security flank, and the Zulu/Z angle, plus sight into Dining. Obvious if defenders spot it but still worth it for the information.

Source audio also named an operator phonetically as 'doabe' for holding this cutoff window — that doesn't match any real Attack-side operator (a same-sounding real name, Doc, is Defense-side and can't be this pick), so treat that mention as unconfirmed. Gridlock is explicitly and confidently named as the alternative/preferred pick for this exact spot.

After securing the White Stairs cutoff window, throw Trax Stingers on White Stairs or on Zulu (covers most of that side) — can drop a second one to cover both, and another behind you once Freezer is taken to cover your own steps.

gadgetPlacements.location: Meeting, on the bookcase then the window ledge/frame
gadgetPlacements.note: Strong pre-placed drone spot — sees both Split doors and the top Meeting door, and can see the hatch. Left here for intel through the execute.

gadgetPlacements.location: Tower stairs (near the Green window entry)
gadgetPlacements.note: Another drone left here to watch for anyone coming up the tower stairs; pairs well with a Gridlock Trax Stinger thrown from the same spot.

Opens the Blue door barricade and the Meeting hatch early as a decoy ('Phantom pressure') to bait defenders into setting up around Blue/Ebox/Elbow/Pillar while the real push goes through Freezer. Source audio is heavily garbled on this operator's name across the transcript, but 'her' (female pronoun used), hard breach charges, and later 'X-KAIRO pellets' description confirm this is Hibana.

gadgetPlacements.location: Freezer reinforced wall (the side defenders usually reinforce, with head holes)
gadgetPlacements.note: Opens the reinforced Freezer wall with a hard breach once Capitão has smoked off the head holes covering it — don't hard-breach into an un-smoked head hole.

gadgetPlacements.location: Freezer box / kill box (aka Laundry box)
gadgetPlacements.note: Fires a flame bolt into the kill box/Laundry box area and onto the Freezer box so nobody can stand there — flame bolts hit the wall and drop to the floor, so aim accordingly.

Smokes off the rotate and the Highway door using crossbow smoke bolts, aimed about halfway up the wall (too low only covers the floor, too high only covers the ceiling). Only two smokes available, so choose between the cross/rotate angle and the Highway door if you can't cover both.

gadgetPlacements.location: Head holes on Ebox / the rotate / Highway (post-breach hold)
gadgetPlacements.note: Two players needed post-breach to watch the Ebox head holes and the rotate simultaneously — one person can't cover both angles. A safer tucked position exists further back from the head holes rather than pushing all the way up.
Holds the Highway cutoff / rotate off once the wall is open, watching for defenders repositioning to get an angle through the same line of sight.

gadgetPlacements.operator: Lion or Nokk (source audio: 'lur'/'backstab', i.e. an operator who can play passively/stay hidden)
gadgetPlacements.note: Fifth operator hangs back near Green, either pushing down the stairs late for a free pick on Highway once the fight is loud elsewhere, or dropping the hatch. Source audio said a name that sounds like 'Lion' or possibly referenced a roam-hiding playstyle ('backstab') rather than a specific operator — creator explicitly says any flex pick works here, so treat the specific operator as the squad's choice rather than a fixed recommendation.

operatorRecommendations.note: Opens the Blue door and Meeting hatch as a decoy first, then moves to Freezer to hard-breach the reinforced wall (after Capitão smokes the head holes covering it), then holds the Highway cutoff.
Crossbow flame bolts deny the Freezer box/kill box; smoke bolts cover the rotate/cross and the Highway door for a safe crossing.
Holds the White Stairs window cutoff, then Trax Stingers to lock down White Stairs/Zulu; strong alternative to the unconfirmed 'doabe' pick for the same role.
One of the two entry players taking Small Tower/Dining/Shower control at the start, later helps hold the Ebox head holes and rotate post-breach.

operatorRecommendations.operator: Flex (5th pick, e.g. a passive/roam-hider or any flex operator)
operatorRecommendations.note: Hangs near Green, either drops the hatch or pushes down the stairs late for free kills on Highway once the main fight has drawn attention elsewhere.

generalTips.note: The stated goal of this whole plan is to avoid the standard Blue-door push, which most/all defending teams anticipate and stack heavily (Melusi, Bandit, Frost/Kapkan-style traps, Mute jammers etc. across three angles: Sho door, Ebox door, and Elbow) — going through Laundry/Freezer instead avoids that chokepoint entirely.

Enter the building via Small Tower (preferred) or T3/top of the building. Small Tower is the only way to reach that side of the map besides one other door, so a single drone there tells you definitively whether anyone is on that side.
Shoot the default camera in the kitchen (near Small Tower) immediately once you have control of that area.

A player should hold the White Stairs window cutoff for roughly 40-50 seconds while the two entry players secure Dining and Shower control — this corridor is constantly patrolled by at least one defender, sometimes reinforced by someone rotating down from top floor.

A separate player shoots down the front door barricade and the default camera there, then holds passively through the van windows (a more secure angle) watching Classroom, some of Garage, Bottom Main, and Zulu/Z for about 30-40 seconds.
Watch out crossing from the Freezer/kill-box side toward the plant spot — there's an angle defenders can hold from behind the washing machine or the nearby wall.

At the bottom of the Freezer stairs, defenders hold an angle from standing on the Freezer box almost 100% of the time — drone it first, then pre-fire that corner on the way down rather than looking elsewhere.
Getting across the doorway from the kill-box area to the plant spot is the hardest part of the whole execute if you don't have smokes — near-impossible without Capitão's smoke coverage.

This attack is rated more complex/coordinated than most of the creator's other single-site attack guides — recommends at least 3, ideally 4-5 players, and practicing it in custom/quick play before using it in ranked.
Still viable (if a bit improvised) with only 3 players — split as two players pushing Freezer and one holding Bottom Main.

Expect defenders to place utility (Fenrir traps, Maloy/Melusi-style banshees, Aruni-style ADS denial, possibly a mirror) concentrated around the Blue door area specifically because it's the most commonly expected push — this is exactly what the decoy hatch/door opening is meant to bait out early.
